channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Las aplicaciones de redes sociales enganchan a los estudiantes en horario escolar
item.description: Documentos internos muestran que empresas como Snap y TikTok mandaban mensajes a sus jóvenes usuarios a lo largo del día, distrayéndolos de sus clases.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Valentino-DeVries

media:content.media:credit: Yana Paskova para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: El empuje de las empresas de redes sociales para llegar a los estudiantes a menudo ha eclipsado las preocupaciones de padres y profesores.

item.title: New York City’s Most Patient Soccer Fans Are Ready for the Party
item.description: Uzbekistan is new to the World Cup. Norway and Haiti haven’t been there in decades. For expat communities all over the city, the wait is nearly over.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Keh and Todd Heisler

media:content.media:description: The great agony of the World Cup, for its most ardent fans, stems from its scarcity, like if Christmas came only every four years.

item.title: A.I. Companies Don’t Know What to Do With Alex Bores
item.description: Outside groups have spent roughly $12 million to support or oppose Mr. Bores’s campaign for a House seat in Manhattan, elevating his name in a crowded race.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Oreskes

media:content.media:credit: Anna Watts for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Assemblyman Alex Bores represents a district on Manhattan’s East Side that lies in the 12th Congressional District, where he is running to succeed Representative Jerrold Nadler.

item.title: How Tony Danza Spends a Day Playing a Villain and Frank Sinatra
item.description: Mr. Danza does not know the meaning of a day off. He said he has too much still to do.
item.dc:creator: Addie Morfoot

media:content.media:credit: Ye Fan for The New York Times

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for June 6, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Trump Pardons Former Congressman Convicted of Insider Trading
item.description: Stephen Buyer, a former Republican representative from Indiana, was convicted of trading stock related to two deals before they were made public.
item.dc:creator: Francesca Regalado

media:content.media:description: Stephen Buyer arriving for his insider trading trial in New York City in 2023.

item.title: China levanta murallas alrededor de su economía
item.description: Pekín afirma que las normas anunciadas esta semana son necesarias para la seguridad nacional, pero podrían complicar los esfuerzos por encontrar crecimiento en el extranjero.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Stevenson and Murphy Zhao

media:content.media:description: Un robot fabricado por una empresa china expuesto en abril en una exposición en Cantón, China. Pekín dio a conocer esta semana normas que ralentizarán la expansión de las empresas chinas en el extranjero.

item.title: Nick es un Jonas Brother y un solista a la vez
item.description: Con un protagónico en la película ‘Letras robadas’ y ambiciones en otros medios, el joven de 33 años se está labrando un camino singular.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Spencer and OK McCausland

item.title: No hay nada malo en desear a los hombres
item.description: El “heteropesimismo” está de moda, pero en realidad nunca ha habido un mejor momento para buscar el amor. Vamos, sé hetero-optimista.
item.dc:creator: Magdalene J. Taylor

item.title: Laboring Under Delhi’s Harsh Heat, Workers Must Choose Health or Wages
item.description: Severe heat waves have been hitting India since April, forcing many of the country’s essential workers to make tough decisions.
item.dc:creator: Pragati K.B., Anupreeta Das and Anindito Mukherjee

media:content.media:description: Vendors setting up large umbrellas to shade their kiosks last month at a flea market near Jama Masjid, a famous mosque in New Delhi.

item.title: Quote of the Day: Despite Different Native Languages, Linemates Speak Hockey in Harmony
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Saturday, June 6, 2026.

item.title: Mali Conflict Escalates as Rebel Alliance Seizes Key Cities
item.description: Jihadists have nearly tripled their attacks on urban areas. Analysts fear an extended standoff could lead to many more civilian deaths.
item.dc:creator: Ruth Maclean

media:content.media:description: A soldier from the Azawad Liberation Front, one of the groups in a powerful insurgent alliance fighting Mali’s ruling junta, walking inside a damaged building in Kidal, Mali, last month.

item.title: A President, His Prime Minister and the Bitter Rift Dividing Senegal
item.description: The two men became president and prime minister by defeating the political old guard in Senegal. Now they are fighting each other.
item.dc:creator: Saikou Jammeh and Ruth Maclean

media:content.media:description: Ousmane Sonko, left, was appointed prime minister by his friend, President Bassirou Diomaye Faye, and then fired.

item.title: Corrections: June 6, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Saturday, June 6, 2026.

item.title: Bronze Age Pervert’s ‘Cosplay’ Masculinity
item.description: “This is an ideology that is born out of fat modernity itself,” the journalist Helen Lewis argues on “The Ezra Klein Show,” where she explains why the online right’s fascination with hypermasculinity is a bored delusion made possible by the safety of modern life.
item.dc:creator: ‘The Ezra Klein Show’

item.title: Trump Greets Farmers in Wisconsin, but Says He Could Be Home Watching TV
item.description: President Trump was in Wisconsin to reassure farmers who have been stung by his tariff policies and rising fuel prices from the war in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Katie Rogers

media:content.media:description: President Trump traveled to western Wisconsin, which is home to large beef, dairy and crop farms, to shore up support for a Republican loyalist running for re-election in a purple district.

item.title: Solitary
item.description: Take a pass at Dan Bodily’s record-breaking themeless puzzle — it’s wide open.
item.dc:creator: Caitlin Lovinger

media:content.media:description: Spread your wings, solvers!

item.title: Amid Mounting Democratic Concern, Platner Says His Past Is Being ‘Weaponized’
item.description: The presumptive Democratic Senate nominee in Maine said the state would have his back in the face of accusations he has denied.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk and Katie Glueck

media:content.media:description: Graham Platner, a Democratic candidate for U.S. Senate, held a campaign event on Friday at a theater in Bar Harbor, Maine, where he told attendees that his state would have his back in the face of “politically motivated” attacks.

item.title: Cuando Jeffrey Epstein necesitaba favores, recurría a este magnate restaurantero
item.description: Stephen Hanson, cuyo imperio incluía Blue Water Grill y Ruby Foo’s, era un amigo devoto y un compinche que ayudaba a manejar y entretener a las mujeres del círculo de Epstein.
item.dc:creator: Kim Severson

item.title: Trump Says Bill Pulte, His New Intelligence Director, Should Slash Staff
item.description: The president suggested that employees who worked for previous Democratic presidents were among those who should be fired.
item.dc:creator: Maggie Haberman
item.category: Office of the Director of National Intelligence

media:content.media:description: Bill Pulte.

item.title: Park Ranger Dies After Falling Into a Crevasse on Mt. McKinley
item.description: Robin Pendery, 33, who was part of a climbing patrol, was near a camp at about 14,000 feet on the mountain in Alaska when she fell, the National Park Service said.
item.dc:creator: Neil Vigdor

media:content.media:description: Mount McKinley in Alaska is the tallest peak in North America.

item.title: SpaceX Has $30 Billion Deal to Provide Google With A.I. Computing Power
item.description: Elon Musk’s rocket company said Google would pay it $920 million a month, as it prepared for its initial public offering.
item.dc:creator: Kate Conger

media:content.media:description: SpaceX’s Starbase offices in Texas. The agreement with Google could help to establish SpaceX as an infrastructure provider for companies that are fiercely competing in the A.I. race.

item.title: Trump Administration Investigating Gender Treatments at Mount Sinai
item.description: A grand jury subpoenaed the health system for information about adolescent patients who had received gender-related care, Mount Sinai said on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Goldstein

media:content.media:credit: Plexi Images/Universal Images Group, via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: The hospital said it was committed “to safeguarding our patients’ privacy” and said it would only share “de-identified” information if it had to.

item.title: Xavier Becerra Advances in California Governor Race
item.description: Mr. Becerra was long dismissed in the contest until the abrupt departure of Eric Swalwell created a surprise path for an experienced Democrat.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Rosenhall

media:content.media:description: Xavier Becerra at a phone banking event in San Jose, Calif., on Saturday.

item.title: Muere Anthony Head, actor en ‘Buffy’ y villano de ‘Ted Lasso’
item.description: El actor británico fue uno de los pilares de influyentes programas de televisión, y alcanzó la fama inicialmente como imagen de Nescafé.
item.dc:creator: Sopan Deb

media:content.media:description: Anthony Head en 2010

item.title: Iran Fired Drones Toward Strait of Hormuz With U.S. Shooting Down at Least 4, U.S. Military Says
item.description: The latest violence between the United States and Iran threatened a cease-fire and risked further stoking tensions in the region.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: Vessels anchored in the Strait of Hormuz as seen from Musandam, Oman, on Tuesday.

item.title: Iran’s Soccer Team Allowed Into U.S. for World Cup, but Many Staff Denied
item.description: After months of doubts over their participation, Iran’s players have received visas for the United States just days before the World Cup begins.
item.dc:creator: Tariq Panja

media:content.media:description: Members of Iran’s national soccer team training in Antalya, Turkey, on Tuesday.

item.title: Patel Said to Have Fired Five F.B.I. Analysts Over Memo on Catholic Extremists
item.description: It is the administration’s latest effort to force out law enforcement personnel it accuses of “weaponizing” the government against President Trump and his supporters.
item.dc:creator: Devlin Barrett

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Kash Patel, the F.B.I. director, speaking at a hearing this year on Capitol Hill. Some former federal law enforcement officials have said law enforcement agencies are being turned into ideologically driven arms of the presidency.

item.title: Ebola Cases May Top 20,000 in Three Months, C.D.C. Says
item.description: Still, the risk to people in the United States remains low, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention said in a report Friday.
item.dc:creator: Apoorva Mandavilli
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

media:content.media:credit: Arlette Bashizi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Health workers in the isolation ward of a newly established Ebola treatment center in Ituri Province in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, the epicenter of the Ebola outbreak, last month.

item.title: See Where the L.A. Mayoral Candidates Have Done Best So Far
item.description: A map of early returns shows where coalitions have formed in the race between Karen Bass, Spencer Pratt and Nithya Raman.
item.dc:creator: Christine Zhang, Alex Lemonides, Caroline Soler, Matthew Bloch and Shawn Hubler

item.title: Pamela Hicks, Lady-in-Waiting to Elizabeth II of Britain, Dies at 97
item.description: The queen’s third cousin, she was a bridesmaid at the royal wedding in 1947, and witnessed firsthand pivotal moments in British history.
item.dc:creator: Alan Cowell
item.category: Elizabeth II, Queen of Great Britain

item.title: Justice Dept. Promises to Drop Trump’s $1.8 Billion Fund
item.description: It was the department’s clearest statement to date that it was pulling back from a plan to use taxpayer money to make payments to people who claimed to have been politically persecuted.
item.dc:creator: Alan Feuer

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Twin filings on Friday are the strongest signal yet that the Justice Department is serious about dropping the idea of the fund.

item.title: Amid Graham Platner Controversy, Where Does the Maine Senate Race Go Next?
item.description: New revelations about Graham Platner have left Democrats anxious.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Medina

media:content.media:description: Graham Platner

item.title: ANWR Oil Draws Few Bids, Despite Trump’s Push for ‘Liquid Gold’
item.description: An auction to drill in the Arctic National Wildlife Refuge ended with just 10 percent of the available land claimed for oil development.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Friedman

media:content.media:credit: Christopher Miller for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Caribou on the tundra of the 1002 area of the Arctic National Wildlife Refuge.

item.title: Judge Strikes Down a Slate of Trump’s Immigration Policies
item.description: Also, job growth is looking healthier. Here’s the latest at the end of Friday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Cullen

media:content.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Annandale Immigration Court in Annandale, Va., last month.

item.title: Mount Everest Guide Missing for 6 Days Is Found Alive
item.description: Dawa Sherpa, a Mount Everest guide, was found alive on Thursday, six days after he went missing.
item.dc:creator: Jackeline Luna

media:content.media:credit: Prabin Ranabhat/Agence France-Presse, via Getty Images

item.title: N.Y.C. Council Speaker Urged to Act After Member’s Anti-Muslim Posts
item.description: Interfaith leaders said City Council Speaker Julie Menin’s lack of response to Councilwoman Inna Vernikov’s Islamophobic statements called the city’s commitment to protecting Muslims into question.
item.dc:creator: Liam Stack and Maya King

media:content.media:description: Councilwoman Inna Vernikov recently questioned whether Muslims praying near a Jewish school in Brooklyn were there as an “intentional” act of intimidation.

item.title: Police Remove Diabetes Experts From Conference for Distributing Critique of Trump Administration
item.description: Several of those escorted out were scheduled to present at the American Diabetes Association conference this weekend.
item.dc:creator: Dani Blum

item.title: Pentagon Cuts 180 Religious Identities From Military Personnel Records
item.description: The new policy, which the Pentagon framed as a largely administrative action, leaves just 31 religious categories to choose from, 22 of which are Christian.
item.dc:creator: John Ismay, Alexandra E. Petri and Aimee Ortiz
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
Mormons (Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-Day Saints)

media:content.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: In a video posted to social media on March 24, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said that the military’s chaplain corps had been “infected by political correctness and secular humanism” under previous administrations.

item.title: 50 Years of People, Passion and Wine
item.description: Since 1976, Duckhorn Vineyards has made wines that capture the spirit of Napa Valley.

item.title: Raphael Warnock Says the Supreme Court Has Done ‘Violence’ to Democracy
item.description: The Democratic senator from Georgia discusses voting rights, the influence of Christianity on politics, and his new book, “The Crooked Places Made Straight: Reflections on the Moral Meaning of America.”

item.title: Jennifer Lopez Says You Must Be Born in NYC to Be a New Yorker in ‘Subway Takes’ Interview
item.description: The actress and singer Jennifer Lopez said in an interview that someone must be born in New York City to be a New Yorker. Not everyone agrees.
item.dc:creator: Emma G. Fitzsimmons

media:content.media:credit: James Devaney/GC Images, via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: In an interview, the actress Jennifer Lopez said even someone who has lived in the city for 50 years wouldn’t be considered a New Yorker.

item.title: A Dark Vision of Masculinity
item.description: Why does the modern, online vision of masculinity feel so “deeply depressing”? In conversation with the journalist Helen Lewis, the Opinion columnist Ezra Klein explains how leading right-wing figures weaponize the word “virtue” to mask a lack of moral and civic obligation.
item.dc:creator: ‘The Ezra Klein Show’

item.title: Northeast Heat Forecast: New York Braces for a Surge of Summer Warmth
item.description: Boston, New York City and Philadelphia are expected to get into the 90s this weekend. More heat is coming next week.
item.dc:creator: Amy Graff

media:content.media:description: Summer heat is settling in to New York and other parts of the Northeast.

item.title: Anthony Head, ‘Buffy the Vampire Slayer’ and ‘Ted Lasso’ Actor, Dies at 72
item.description: The British actor was a mainstay of influential television shows who first found fame as a Nescafe pitchman.
item.dc:creator: Sopan Deb

media:content.media:description: Anthony Head in 2010.

item.title: TK
item.description: TK

item.title: How the Richest Owner in Sports Covered His Tracks
item.description: The latest chapter in Pablo’s Kawhi-Gate investigation.
item.dc:creator: Pablo Torre, Walter Avaroma, Maxwell Carney, Ryan Cortes, Juan Galindo, Patrick Kim, Neely Lohmann, Rob McRae, Clare Taylor, Chris Tumminello and Matt Sullivan

item.title: Trump Arch Will Need Red Lights, F.A.A. Says
item.description: The Federal Aviation Administration advised that if the Trump administration adds red obstruction lights to the arch design, it is likely to pose no risk to local aviation.
item.dc:creator: Karoun Demirjian
item.category: White House East Wing and Ballroom (Washington, DC)

media:content.media:description: The F.A.A. says the proposed 259-foot tall arch to be built on Memorial Circle in Washington would likely not pose aviation safety risks.

item.title: First U.S. Screwworm Case Since 1960s Prompts Texas Emergency Response
item.description: The infections are the first detected in the United States since the 1960s. Officials are aggressively trying to prevent the parasite from spreading in the nation’s largest cattle-producing state.
item.dc:creator: Edgar Sandoval

media:content.media:description: Officials are dispersing millions of sterile screwworm flies in the vicinity of where the infected calf was found.

item.title: A Sherpa Survived 6 Days Alone on Everest. His Family Says He Was Abandoned.
item.description: Dawa Sherpa, 57, was found alive on Thursday, nearly a week after he was last seen on the mountain. His wife says more could have been done to find him sooner.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Kwai and Binod Ghimire

media:content.media:credit: Prabin Ranabhat/Agence France-Presse, via Getty Images
